title: 10 medicinal constituents of Citrus × aurantium (bitter orange)

constituents_list.constituent_desc: Citrus × aurantium synephrine is a naturally occurring alkaloid compound found in the peel and pulp of the fruit. It has been traditionally used in traditional medicine for its stimulating properties, including increasing heart rate and blood pressure. Synephrine is similar to ephedrine, another popular stimulant, but with a more gentle effect on the body.

Citrus × aurantium hesperidin is a flavonoid compound extracted from its leaves and fruits. It has been traditionally used in traditional medicine for its various therapeutic benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antioxidant properties. Hesperidin is also known to have cardiovascular protective effects by reducing blood pressure and improving lipid profiles. Its potential uses extend beyond medicine, with applications in food preservation and as a natural food additive.

Citrus × aurantium naringenin is a bioactive compound found in the peel of this citrus fruit. It has been extensively studied for its potential health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Naringenin has been shown to have a positive effect on cardiovascular health by improving blood lipid profiles and inhibiting platelet aggregation, making it a promising agent for the prevention and treatment of chronic diseases.

Citrus × aurantium aurantiamarin is a bioactive compound extracted from its peel. It has been traditionally used in traditional medicine and aromatherapy for its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Aurantiamarin has been shown to exhibit potential therapeutic effects, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting antiviral activity against certain viruses. Further research is needed to fully understand its mechanisms of action and efficacy.

Citrus × aurantium limonene is a naturally occurring compound found in its peel and leaves. This terpene is responsible for the characteristic scent and flavor of citrus fruits and has been used in traditional medicine and perfumery for centuries. Limonene has also been researched for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

Citrus × aurantium pinene is a type of essential oil that is extracted from the fruit peels of this species. It is a natural compound that has been used for centuries in traditional medicine and aromatherapy due to its numerous health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. Pinene is also known for its refreshing and uplifting aroma, making it a popular ingredient in perfumes, skincare products, and cleaning solutions.

Citrus × aurantium camphor is a natural oil extracted from the peel of the plant. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine and aromatherapy to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues, anxiety, and stress. The oil's invigorating scent and antiseptic properties make it popular in skincare products, while its insect-repelling properties make it a natural pest control solution.

Citrus × aurantium citronellal is a bioactive compound extracted from the peel and leaves of this citrus species. It has been traditionally used in aromatherapy, skincare, and perfumery due to its unique fragrance and potential therapeutic properties. Citronellal has been reported to possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal activities, making it a popular ingredient in various natural products.

Citrus × aurantium linalool is a naturally occurring compound extracted from its peel. It has been traditionally used in aromatherapy and perfumery for its calming and uplifting properties. Linalool has a sweet, citrusy scent and is often used to promote relaxation, reduce anxiety, and improve sleep quality. Additionally, it has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a popular ingredient in skincare products.

Citrus × aurantium geraniol is a natural fragrance compound extracted from the peel of the plant. It has a sweet, floral, and citrusy scent, often used in perfumes, soaps, and aromatherapy products. Geraniol has several therapeutic properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects, making it a popular ingredient in skincare and haircare products. Its aroma is also known to uplift and calm the mind and body.

intro: Citrus × aurantium has active constituents such as flavonoids, limonoids, and alkaloids. These bioactive compounds are responsible for its medicinal properties, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The constituents of bitter orange have been shown to improve cardiovascular health by reducing cholesterol levels and blood pressure, as well as alleviate symptoms of depression and anxiety through their modulatory effects on serotonin and dopamine receptors.
intro_desc: Citrus × aurantium has active constituents such as flavonoids, limonoids, and synephrine. These constituents contribute to its medicinal properties, including stimulant, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. For instance, flavonoids like naringenin and hesperidin can help improve cardiovascular health by reducing blood pressure and cholesterol levels, while limonoids may aid in weight management and reduce cancer cell growth. Synephrine can also boost energy and enhance mental clarity.
